game_info.title: Horizon Racer: Mexico
game_info.concept:
Explore the vibrant open world landscapes of Mexico with limitless, fun driving action in the world’s greatest cars.

Forza Horizon 5 Version Comparison


About the Game

Your Ultimate Horizon Adventure awaits! Explore the vibrant and ever-evolving open world landscapes of Mexico with limitless, fun driving action in hundreds of the world’s greatest cars.


This is Your Horizon Adventure
Lead breathtaking expeditions across the vibrant and ever-evolving open world landscapes of Mexico with limitless, fun driving action in hundreds of the world’s greatest cars.


This is a Diverse Open World
Explore a world of striking contrast and beauty. Discover living deserts, lush jungles, historic cities, hidden ruins, pristine beaches, vast canyons and a towering snow-capped volcano.


This is an Adventurous Open World
Immerse yourself in a deep campaign with hundreds of challenges that reward you for engaging in the activities you love. Meet new characters and choose the outcomes of their Horizon Story missions.


This is an Evolving Open World
Take on awe-inspiring weather events such as towering dust storms and intense tropical storms as Mexico’s unique, dynamic seasons change the world every week. Keep coming back for new events, challenges, collectibles, and rewards, and new areas to explore. No two seasons will ever be the same.


This is a Social Open World
Team up with other players and enter the Horizon Arcade for a continuing series of fun, over-the-top challenges that keep you and your friends in the action and having fun with no menus, loading screens or lobbies. Meet new friends in Horizon Open and Tours and share your creations with new community gift sharing.


This is Your Open World
Create your own expressions of fun with the powerful new EventLab gameplay toolset including custom races, challenges, stunts, and entirely new game modes. Customize your cars in more ways than ever before with new options such as the ability open and close convertible tops, paint brake calipers, and more. Use the new Gift Drops feature to share your custom creations with the community.


Begin Your Horizon Adventure today!
game_info.description: Welcome to Horizon Racer: Mexico! Experience the thrill of open-world driving across vibrant Mexican landscapes. Race through diverse terrain including deserts, jungles, beaches, and mountain passes. Collect speed boost tokens scattered across the map to increase your velocity. Navigate checkpoints to earn points and unlock new areas. Avoid obstacles like rocks and trees that will slow you down. Your goal is to explore the beautiful world, collect 50 tokens, and reach a score of 1000 points to complete your ultimate horizon adventure!
game_info.controls:
W or Up Arrow: Accelerate forward
S or Down Arrow: Brake/Reverse
A or Left Arrow: Steer left
D or Right Arrow: Steer right
Space: Handbrake/Drift
Shift: Activate speed boost (when available)
Enter: Start game
ESC: Pause/Resume
R: Restart game

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic vehicle movement and controls. This validates that the car responds correctly to acceleration, steering, and braking inputs. Ensures physics feel responsive and natural.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Drive in a figure-8 pattern for 15 seconds, alternating between acceleration and braking. Test sharp turns using handbrake. Verify the car maintains stable physics and doesn't glitch through terrain.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The vehicle should smoothly accelerate, decelerate, and turn without physics errors. Steering should feel responsive. The car should stay grounded on terrain. Test passes if vehicle completes figure-8 without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing collectible token system and scoring mechanics. Validates that tokens spawn correctly, can be collected, and properly increment score. Tests the boost activation system.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Drive directly toward visible tokens for 20 seconds, collecting as many as possible. Activate boost after collecting 3 tokens. Monitor score increases and boost availability. Check token respawn mechanics.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Tokens should be collected on contact, incrementing score by 10 points each. Boost gauge should fill with collections and activate correctly. Score should reach at least 50 points. Test passes when 5+ tokens collected successfully.

TEST_2.expected: Tokens should be collected on contact, incrementing score by 10 points each. Boost gauge should fill with collections and activate correctly. Score should reach at least 50 points. Test passes when 5+ tokens collected successfully.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing checkpoint navigation system. Validates checkpoint spawning, detection, and the progression through multiple checkpoints. Tests the waypoint guidance system.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Follow the checkpoint markers for 25 seconds, attempting to drive through at least 3 checkpoints. Observe the guidance arrow and checkpoint indicators. Test checkpoint sequence progression.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Checkpoints should appear with clear visual indicators. Driving through a checkpoint should award 50 points and spawn the next checkpoint. Arrow should guide player to active checkpoint. Test passes when 3 checkpoints completed.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing collision detection with obstacles and terrain boundaries. Validates that the vehicle properly collides with rocks, trees, and world boundaries. Tests slowdown mechanics on impact.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Deliberately drive into various obstacles including rocks and trees for 15 seconds. Test boundary collision at world edges. Monitor speed reduction on impacts. Verify no glitches through objects.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Collisions should slow the vehicle down appropriately. Car should not pass through solid obstacles. Boundary walls should keep vehicle within playable area. Test passes if all collision types work correctly without physics errors.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing camera follow system and third-person controls. Validates smooth camera movement, proper target tracking, and view stability during high-speed maneuvers.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Perform various driving maneuvers including sharp turns, drifts, and speed changes for 20 seconds. Test camera behavior during rapid direction changes. Verify camera doesn't clip through terrain.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Camera should smoothly follow the vehicle with slight lag for cinematic feel. View should remain stable during all maneuvers. Camera should never clip through terrain or obstacles. Test passes if camera maintains proper framing throughout.
TEST_5.description: Testing camera follow system and third-person controls. Validates smooth camera movement, proper target tracking, and view stability during high-speed maneuvers.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing terrain generation and biome diversity. Validates that the procedural world includes varied terrain types including desert, jungle, and beach areas with appropriate visual styling.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Drive in a large circular pattern around the map for 30 seconds, traversing different biome areas. Observe terrain color changes and environmental variety. Check for visual consistency and proper biome transitions.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Should encounter at least 3 distinct biome types with different color palettes. Terrain should have visual variety with different ground colors. Transitions between biomes should be smooth. Test passes when multiple biomes observed.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing win condition by collecting 50 tokens and reaching 1000 points. Validates complete gameplay loop from start to victory. Tests game-over-win state transition and UI display.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Play optimally for 60 seconds, focusing on collecting tokens and completing checkpoints. Use boosts strategically. Attempt to reach 1000 points by combining token collection (10 pts each) and checkpoint completion (50 pts each).
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Upon reaching 1000 points and collecting 50 tokens, game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state. Victory screen should display final score and collection count. Test passes when win condition triggers correctly with proper UI feedback.
